headline: Snowflake blows past estimates, lifts FY27 guide and signs $6B AWS pact; shares surge ~37%
summary: Snowflake reported Q1 FY27 product revenue of $1.33B, up 34% YoY — its strongest sequential dollar growth ever — with total revenue of $1.39B (+33%) and adjusted EPS of $0.39 versus the ~$0.32 consensus. Net revenue retention held at 126% and remaining performance obligations reached $9.21B (+38%). Management raised full-year product-revenue guidance to $5.84B (from $5.66B) and disclosed a five-year, $6B commitment to AWS to expand AI and data workloads. Shares jumped roughly 37% premarket and a string of desks lifted targets into the $272–$325 range.

headline: Salesforce beats and unveils $25B buyback, but soft Q2 guide and AI skepticism cap the stock
summary: Salesforce posted record Q1 FY27 revenue of $11.1B (+13% YoY) and non-GAAP EPS of $3.88 (+50%), with Agentforce ARR reaching $1.2B (up more than 200% YoY) and combined AI + Data Cloud ARR of $3.4B. It raised the FY27 revenue-guidance midpoint to $45.9–46.2B and launched a $25B accelerated share repurchase (103M shares delivered upfront). However, Q2 revenue guidance of $11.27–11.35B sat just below the ~$11.36B consensus, and several desks trimmed targets, leaving shares modestly lower premarket. CRM is down roughly 33% year-to-date.

headline: Marvell posts record quarter and raises AI outlook, but shares slip on 'sell-the-news'
summary: Marvell reported record Q1 FY27 revenue of $2.418B (+28% YoY) and non-GAAP EPS of $0.80, and guided Q2 to ~$2.70B (+35% YoY) with growth expected to accelerate each quarter on datacenter strength. Management cited exceptional AI bookings, raised its FY27 and FY28 revenue outlooks, and flagged more than 20 custom-silicon (XPU) design wins entering production in FY28–29 across hyperscalers including AWS Trainium and Azure. Despite the beat-and-raise, shares fell about 3% premarket after doubling in 2026 — a mixed read-through for the custom-AI-silicon complex (AVGO, ANET).

headline: Dell reports after the close today with AI-server margins in focus
summary: Dell reports Q1 FY27 results after today's close, with consensus near $35.2B revenue (~+51% YoY) and ~$2.90 non-GAAP EPS. Investors are watching whether the AI-optimized server backlog — $43B entering FY27 after more than $64B booked in FY26 — sets another record and prompts a guidance raise, and crucially whether margins hold as GPU- and memory-heavy systems dilute profitability amid the ongoing memory supercycle. The stock has risen sharply in 2026.

headline: HP Inc tops estimates as AI PCs drive 13% Personal Systems growth despite higher component costs
summary: HP Inc reported fiscal Q2 2026 revenue of $14.4B (+9% YoY) and non-GAAP EPS of $0.86 (+21%), with Personal Systems revenue up 13% on double-digit growth in AI PCs, advanced compute and commercial demand. Personal Systems operating margin of 5.2% beat expectations as the company accelerated mitigation of higher input costs — a notable signal given the memory-price supercycle pressuring DRAM/NAND buyers. The print is a positive read-through for AI-PC demand and component-cost management across the hardware chain (MU, Samsung, SK Hynix).

headline: Futures soft as US-Iran tensions lift oil and a hot April inflation print tempers risk appetite
summary: Equity futures were under mild pressure premarket as renewed US-Iran tensions pushed crude higher — WTI up roughly 1.8% near $90 and Brent near $96 — and an April PCE reading (headline +0.4% MoM, 3.8% YoY, per TheStreet) kept inflation worries in view. The backdrop is a modest headwind for rate-sensitive AI/datacenter growth names even as single-stock earnings dominate the tape, with the prior session leaving the S&P 500 roughly flat and the Dow lagging.
